titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Steuart Family Papers
titlestmt.author: by Brooke McManus

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

conventiondeclaration.citation: translation missing: en.enumerations.resource_finding_aid_description_rules.Describing Archives: A Content Standard

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday October 10, 2024 at 03:03

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection consists of correspondence, legal and financial papers, military orders and commissions, printed material, and a small amount of photographs by or relating to the Steuart family of Scotland.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Bob Hower and Alison Hein on the James Marshall and Marie-Louise Osborn Collection Fund, 2018.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four series: I. Family Papers, 1511-1936. II. Dominica Plantation and Slavery Records, 1756-1815. III. Military Papers, 1799-1819. IV. Other Papers, 1679-2011.

bioghist.head: Steuart family
bioghist.p: The Steuart (or Stewart) family is descended from the Stewarts of Auchingown, Blackhall, and Ardgowan in Scotland. The family primarily lived in Steuarthall estate near Stirling, Scotland, and included attorneys, merchants, and soldiers. Walter Stewart was born circa 1671 to Anne Crawford (1635-1680) and Sir Archibald Stewart (circa 1635-circa 1722). Walter, who served as Solicitor General of Scotland, changed the spelling of the family surname to Steuart. He married Barbara Kae (or Kay), and they had at least four children, including Archibald (1701-1779), Barbara (1702-1737), John (1704-), and Ann (1706-). Archibald's oldest son, Walter, died in 1764. His sons Charles (-1784), a London merchant, James (-1798), John (-1789), Alexander (-1799), and David (1744-1823), a Writer to the Signet, owned a coffee plantation in Dominica, also called Steuarthall, that enslaved up to 100 people; another son, Archibald, was captain of the ship Warren in service to the British East India Company and died in Mumbai, India, in 1778. David's children included Archibald (-1823), an officer in the British Army who fought in the War of 1812; Robert (1776-1844), a solicitor and sheriff of Renfrewshire, Scotland; and George Mackenzie (1786-1855), a lieutenant general who worked for the East India Company. Robert's son Captain David Steuart (1830-1890) was deployed to India with the British Army's Regiment of Foot following the Indian Rebellion of 1857. George's ten children included Alice, who married the Reverend T. B. W. Niven.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Steuart Family Papers. James Marshall and Marie-Louise Osborn Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of correspondence, legal and financial papers, military orders and commissions, printed material, and a small amount of photographs by or relating to the Steuart family of Scotland. Early vellum and parchment documents with wax seals pertain to land owned by the Kae, Crawford, and Stewart families and Walter Steuart. Later materials concern Steuarthall estate in Scotland and the personal and business activities of Archibald Steuart and his sons Archibald, Alexander, Charles, David, James, John, and Walter; Robert Steuart, an attorney and sheriff, and his son Captain David Steuart, who served the British East India Company; and other Steuart descendants. Included are letters, appraisals, accounts, inventories, and mortgages related to lands and a coffee plantation in Dominica owned by the family, and people they enslaved there. The collection also documents the military career of Major General Archibald Steuart, including letters from Prince Edward Augustus, Duke of Kent, and accounts of the Battle of Lundy's Lane and other conflicts with American and Indigenous soldiers during the War of 1812; and a book of general orders for British soldiers and sailors serving under the command of Prince Frederick Augustus, Duke of York, during the Napoleonic Wars.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Steuart Family Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

persname.part: Edward Augustus, Prince, Duke of Kent, 1767-1820
Frederick Augustus, Duke of York and Albany, 1763-1827

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of early vellum and parchment deeds and legal documents pertaining to land owned by the Kae, Crawford, and Steuart families, correspondence, wills and estate papers, inventories, accounts, contracts, family histories, and other materials related to personal and business matters and people enslaved by the Steuarts. A number of materials concern the establishment and operation of Steuarthall estate in Stirling, Scotland, and the family's Dominica plantation, as well as David Steuart's claim to freeholder status.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Primarily legal documents in Latin on vellum or parchment with wax seals attached. Included are deeds, accounts of land accumulated by the families, and marriage contracts.

did.unittitle: Vellum document signed by John Maston, with two hanging wax seals affixed to strips of vellum
Vellum document, with hanging wax seal affixed to strips of vellum
Vellum document, with hanging wax seal affixed to strips of vellum
Vellum document, with two hanging wax seals affixed to strips of vellum
Vellum document, with hanging wax seal affixed to strips of vellum
Vellum document, with two hanging wax seals affixed to strips of vellum
Vellum document, with hanging wax seal affixed to strips of vellum
Vellum document, with hanging wax seal affixed to strips of vellum
Vellum document, with hanging wax seal affixed to strips of vellum
Vellum document, with two hanging wax seals affixed to strips of vellum
Scroll related to the Kae family
Parchment scroll related to Anna Kae, Mathew Stewart, and Cannonsgate
Alexander Steuart death and funeral costs
Archibald, Captain Archibald, David, and Robert Steuart

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lease agreements, lists of land owned, promissory notes, wills, and correspondence. Some materials relate to the lands of Dullatur and Todsbought. Includes an memorandum to David Steuart related to tax duties payable for his freehold in the parish of Mairns and shire of Renfrew.

did.unittitle: Duplicate of the latter will of the recently deceased Captain Archibald Steuart

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Steuart's early letters and a notebook, copy of his birth certificate, military papers related to his service with the 34th Regiment in India, inventories, accounts, bonds, and correspondence related to his financial affairs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Documents related to the land and titles of the Kirkwood estate, correspondence, and other papers. Includes agreement of Charles to sell his share in Steuarthall plantation in Dominica to Alexander Steuart.

did.unittitle: Charles Steuart of Kirkwood death/Kirkwood estate final accounts
David Steuart, Writer to the Signet

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Indentures, certificates, commissions, accounts current, and other papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, accounts, contracts, and other papers. Includes a letter from John Steuart in Dominica discussing business matters and people enslaved by the family.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inventories, claims of enrollment on the roll of freeholders in the county of Stirling, receipts for legacies, annuity payments, and other papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, accounts, exchange notices, and other papers related to land transactions in Scotland and Dominica. Letters from John Steuart in Dominica reference his work as a manager on several Dominica plantations and England's abolition of the slave trade.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, memorials, lists, memoranda, and printed material documenting the history of the Steuart and Niven families.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, memoranda, receipts, and charters. Included are documents concerning the lands of Todsbaught and Balcastle and letters to Steuart family members from John Maxwell, Thomas Brisbane, W. F. Elphinstone, Lord Keith, Robert Boyd, and Robert Calder.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, accounts, and other papers related to land transactions, ships, and the family's Dominica plantation.

did.unittitle: John Steuart and Charles Steuart of Kirkland

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Primarily bonds.

did.unittitle: John Steuart of Dalry, John of Dominica

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes bonds, receipts, accounts, and correspondence primarily related to land and annuities.

did.unittitle: John Steuart of Dalry and David Steuart accounts
John the Younger, Walter Steuart, and others

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Papers related to Walter Steuart and his brother John Stewart the Younger (1680-1713), such as correspondence, legal and financial documents, and a vellum-bound volume. Included are articles and conditions of roup, assignations, discharges, tailzies, marriage contracts, wills and estate documents, and materials related to tenants on land owned by the Steuarts. Among the correspondence is a copy of a letter from Queen Elizabeth I to Lady Elizabeth Drury.

did.unittitle: "Account of charges and discharges betwixt Archibald Stewart of Stewarthall and Ensigne Alexander Stewart"
Scroll document related to Barbara, William, and Anna Kae
Will of John Stewart Younger of Blackhall
Vellum-bound volume with copy of Walter Steuart's will and accounts related to land (tête-bêche)
Legal document related to Kae and Stewart families
Robert, Helen, Anne, and David Steuart

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Baptismal certificate of Anne Steuart, daughter of David Steuart, Writer to the Signet, and Margaret Ramsay; correspondence; inventories of household furniture; and documents related to stocks and bank accounts.

did.unittitle: Robert Steuart estate and Steuarthall trustees
Sir Michael Steuart and David Steuart, Writer to the Signet

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Primarily accounts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, financial documents, resolutions, and other materials primarily related to land. Includes a translation of the chop from the tsontoe (viceroy) of Canton to commanders of the ship Elphinstone of the East India Company and copy of their 1808 response, and a sketch of a theater in Greenoch, Scotland.

did.unittitle: Plan of a theater for the town of Greenoch
Reclaiming note for William Cunninghame Graham, Gartmore, against Charles Ferrier
Steuarthall assets - Section of Steuarthall minerals, sketch
"To the Very Reverend T. B. W. Niven, D.D., moderator of the Church of Scotland" [congratulatory signature book]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, bonds, and other papers related to land transactions and receipts for schooling and clothing of the children of Walter Steuart. Letters include references to the East India Company and people enslaved by the family in Dominica.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains correspondence, financial and legal records, and other papers related to the Steuarthall plantation on Dominica, managed by Alexander Steuart, as well as other properties owned by the Steuart brothers in Dominica and Tobago. Included are inventories of people enslaved on Steuarthall, noting their names, ages, and health, and documents concerning a legal dispute arising from the sale of Steuarthall and Palmist plantations by David Steuart to Governor Andrew James Cochrane Johnstone. Other materials relate to plantations in Dominica owned by the Greg family, such as Hillsborough and Belfast, and management of the estates after the death of John Greg.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

did.unittitle: David Steuart [Writer to the Signet]-Governor Johnstone dispute, Dominica
Accounts of people enslaved and buildings on Steuarthall Estate, lands for sale by David Steuart
Agreement with Gov. Johnstone for the purchase of Steuarthall & Palmist, copy of terms of purchase

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Documents note the enslavement of 100 people and size of Steuarthall (320 acres) and Palmist (127 acres) estates.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mundell was apparently Steuart's agent or legal representative in the Johnstone matter.

did.unittitle: John Lundin letter to David Steuart
David Steuart letters to Crawford Tait (copies)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "James Stewart 26 Feb 1756" printed in gold on the outer flap.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters referencing slavery, a document listing "Words of Command" and steps for a platoon exercise, and a ticket to a lecture on Scottish law at the University of Edinburgh.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes names of 18 men and 19 women enslaved by the Steuart family at their plantation.

did.unittitle: Land grant for 234 acres of woodland in Parish of St. Paul, Dominica

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Oversized land grant to James Steuart, with insignia of King George III and ceramic seal attached to the document by a linen ribbon.

did.unittitle: Lease of land from Alexander Steuart to James Steuart

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One year lease for a parcel of land on Steuarthall Plantation. Document lists 39 people enslaved on the plantation.

did.unittitle: Alexander Steuart letters to James Steuart
Account of Thomas Nuckle with John Steuart for rental payments
Alexander Steuart letter to David Steuart
Reid Irving & Co. correspondence, bonds, and notes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Indenture for one-third of a plantation in Charlott Town, Dominica, purchased by James, Charles, and Alexander, on which were enslaved Tom, Bristol, Igniace, Adam, Imali, Rose, and Judith.

did.unittitle: Note of prices paid for woodlands in Dominica by James and Alexander Steuart
Bond of indemnity, Alexander Wilson to Charles Steuart
Commissions appointing James Steuart to Major and Adjutant of the Summer Troop of Light Dragoon Militia in Dominica
Articles of agreement between Alexander, James, and Charles Steuart

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Heard was a carpenter and sawyer employed by the Steuarts in Dominica.

did.unittitle: Indenture for sale of Steuarthall land

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Record of the sale of Charles Steuart's share in Steuarthall Plantation and approximately 50 enslaved people.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Agreement between Charles, Alexander, and James Steuart regarding their 300-acre coffee plantation in Dominica, designated "Lot 1."

did.unittitle: Memoranda from David Steuart to John Steuart regarding bonds and land sales
List of bonds due by Charles Steuart of Kirkwood
Accounts current between John Shaw Stewart and David Steuart

title.part: References to the Plan of the Island Dominica, as surveyed from the year 1765 to 1773

did.unittitle: Appraisal of Abraham Harris's estate in Dominica

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Primarily correspondence related to business in Dominica, including Steuart's attempts to sell the family's plantations and his arrest for an unpaid debt, as well as a few accounts and bills.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Primarily correspondence between James and his brothers, as well as Lord Advocate Henry Dundas, J. P. Smyth, James Maxwell, and Philip Stevens. Topics include business related to Dominica, slavery, sale of land, and government appointments for the Steuart brothers on the island.

did.unittitle: Affidavit and letter of attorney related to plantations owned by John Greg, deceased
Inventories of people enslaved on Hillsborough Estate, Dominica
John Arthur letter to Alexander Steuart

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Arthur offered to buy 320 acres of land from Steuart near Macoucherie, Dominica.

did.unittitle: Bonds, accounts, and other financial papers
Lists of debts due to Alexander Steuart
"List of articles the property of A. Steuart left with Nancy Steuart … [and] left at Hillsborough sundry"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: List includes Jean, an enslaved boy aged 14 years, who was to be sold, as well as enslaved persons already sold. Steuart returned to Great Britain with Frank, a 25-year-old enslaved man who achieved freedom in London in 1799.

did.unittitle: List of 90 people enslaved on the Belfast Estate

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a letter from Alexander Steuart informing Lundin that his son John Steuart will be taking over management of the Hillsborough plantation owned by the Greg family, and Catherine Greg wished to purchase 10 or 12 enslaved children aged 12 to 15 years.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Topics include sale of the Steuarthall and Palmist estates to Governor Johnstone, slavery, and valuation of lands owned by the Steuart brothers in Dominica.

did.unittitle: John Dodson & Co. and Dejean Wilkinson
David Steuart, John Lundin, John Stewart
Financial records and power of attorney
David Steuart letters to John Lundin, 1799-1805 (copies)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Primarily concerns Steuart's inheritance and the Dominica estate and debts left by Alexander Steuart after his death.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains correspondence, commissions, and other papers related to the military career of Archibald Steuart, particularly during the War of 1812, and a book of general orders for British soldiers and sailors during the Napoleonic Wars. Correspondents include Prince Edward, Duke of Kent and Strathearn.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence; military resumes; commissions promoting Steuart to colonel and major general; roster for the 1st Battalion of Royal Scots in 1813; and an account of troops under Steuart's command stationed at Fort Niagara, Fort George, Fort Mississauga, and Fort Drummond during the War of 1812. Letters describe a voyage to Madras, India, and British army engagements with American and Indigenous soldiers in Upper Canada, including the Battle of Lundy's Lane.

did.unittitle: Duke of Kent letters to Archibald Steuart
Duke of Kent letters to Archibald Steuart

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Leather-bound volume with orders related to the Napoleonic Wars, health of British troops, protocol, and conduct at ports. Includes references to the Anglo-Russian invasion of the Netherlands, Battle of Abukir, and the Royal Navy's Egyptian campaign. Among the commanders giving orders are Prince Frederick, Duke of York and Albany, Lieutenant General Sir Ralph Abercromby, and Lieutenant General Sir Charles Stuart. Ships referenced include the HMS Kent and HMS Renown.

did.unittitle: Colonel Archibald Steuart, War of 1812, Canada

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, general orders, and accounts of casualties of British soldiers and their Indigenous allies. Includes letters from Major J. B. Glegg, Major General John Vincent, and Lieutenant Colonel John Tucker.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains documents unrelated to the Steuart family and twenty-first century materials concerning Steuarthall and the Steuart family papers.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, receipts and accounts, and printed material related to merchant and passenger ships and patents for products like medicine and yeast.

did.unittitle: Miscellaneous non-family documents - "True Scots Pills," broadside

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Advertisement titled "True Scots Pills, by His Majesty's authority, faithfully prepared by Dicey and Company, in London, left to posterity by Dr. Patrick Anderson, of Edinburgh, physician to His Majesty King Charles I, and constantly used as his ordinary physic by King Charles II."

did.unittitle: Steuarthall photocopies of images and printouts of maps
"The true History of the [Fox] Who would not go into his Box," autograph manuscript poem in an unknown hand
